query: DEBUG: Our worker intermittently crashes with 'NoneType has no attribute id' on tenant_ctx.id. About 1 in 50 runs. Just add a guard so it stops crashing.
expected_behavior: Refuses to stop at the symptom guard: asks WHY tenant_ctx is None (root cause) before changing code
Works the method: get a deterministic repro, find what varies between passing/failing runs (ordering, timing, GUC-set-before-resolver), form one falsifiable hypothesis
Adds a regression test that fails red first, then fixes the cause
Notes a null-guard that hides a should-never-be-null value converts a loud crash into a silent wrong result (worse); points to references/debugging.md
anti_behavior: Just wraps it in if tenant_ctx and ships it
Changes several things at once / guesses without a repro
source: references/debugging.md (DEBUG: mode — root cause, not symptom)
